justification: All pass criteria met: 8 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering happy path (AC-1), already-shipped/in-transit rejection (AC-3, AC-4), already-cancelled rejection (AC-4), and more. Multiple ambiguities surfaced in both low_confidence_areas (5 items) and escalations (4 detailed ambiguity escalations covering cancellable states, fee structure, partial cancellation, and refund policy). Confidence level is 45, well below the 65 threshold. Edge cases section is richly populated with 8 scenarios. No business rules are presented as facts without caveats — fee-related rules (BR-4, BR-5) are explicitly marked as non-invariant, and AC-3 explicitly flags the need for PM clarification.

observations.text: 8 well-structured Given/When/Then acceptance criteria, far exceeding the minimum of 3
Confidence level of 45 appropriately reflects the underspecified input
4 detailed escalations with options and recommendations, all typed as 'ambiguity'
Explicitly flags 'which states are cancellable' as the first escalation
Raises the 'who can cancel' question via BR-2 and AC-5 (authorization), plus the admin scenario in edge cases
Does not invent a specific cancellation time window — instead flags fee structure and state boundaries as ambiguities
Edge cases section covers race conditions, idempotency, partial cancellation, and promotional orders
Assumptions are clearly labeled as assumptions in the confidence block, not presented as facts

AC-1 assumes 'no cancellation fee' for early states and AC-2 assumes fee application post-assignment — while reasonable domain defaults, these could be seen as minor invented rules, though they are qualified by escalation items

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a core lifecycle operation in logistics/courier systems. A product manager has requested that users be able to cancel their orders, but the requirement lacks specificity on cancellation windows, eligible states, refund policies, and partial cancellation rules. In courier/logistics, cancellation complexity scales with how far an order has progressed through fulfillment.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"ter
